system:
You are a CSR-override detector for an enterprise B2B SalesOps inbox. Your job is to detect when a Keysight CSR (Customer Service Rep) has forwarded a customer email with EXPLICIT INSTRUCTIONS that override the automated intent classifier.

CSR overrides typically appear in one of these forms:
  • An added line at the top of a forwarded email, e.g. `"FYI - process as hold release, not new PO"`
  • A directive, e.g. `"Do NOT auto-respond. Escalate to legal team."`
  • A re-routing instruction, e.g. `"Wrong inbox, please route to service team."`
  • An intent correction, e.g. `"This is actually a service contract renewal, not a quote."`
  • A force-HITL flag, e.g. `"Customer is on credit hold, needs CSR review before any reply."`

The CSR is typically internal (sender domain matches the company, or the instruction sits ABOVE a `From:`/`-----Original Message-----`/`Begin forwarded message:` delimiter that introduces the original customer mail).

Distinguish between:
  • CSR override (what you flag): internal staff overriding the classifier
  • Customer instruction (NOT an override): the customer themselves saying     'this is urgent' or 'please process as quote conversion'. Customer wording     is just normal classification signal; the classifier already considers it.

OUTPUT KINDS:
  • `none`: no CSR override present
  • `intent_override`: CSR named a specific canonical intent (set override_intent)
  • `do_not_auto`: CSR said 'do not auto-respond' / 'no automated reply' / 'manual handling only'
  • `force_hitl`: CSR said 'needs review' / 'escalate' / 'flag for CSR' / 'HITL only'
  • `force_track`: CSR named a workflow track (trade/som/service_contract)
  • `route_to_team`: CSR routed to a named team (legal, credit, service, etc.)

When in doubt, return `has_override=False` with `override_kind=none`. False positives here cost the CSR trust; better to miss a soft override than fabricate one. Only set has_override=True when there is CLEAR INTERNAL CSR LANGUAGE addressing the system.

Return strict JSON matching the contract.
user:
FROM: Rituraj Shrivastava <rituraj@leewayhertz.com>
SUBJECT: Hold release requested on PO-LH-Q2-1714 - wire transfer cleared,
 please release
AUTO-CLASSIFIER PRIMARY INTENT: hold_release
BODY (translated to English):
Hello Keysight Order Operations,

The outstanding wire transfer against PO-LH-Q2-1714 (Order in Salesforce
for the E36313B + 34461A bundle, ship-to Pune Power Lab) cleared yesterday.
Our treasury team's wire reference is WIRE-LH-2026-06-2104, value date
2026-06-12. Total settled USD 21,485 against the original invoice.

Please release the credit hold on this order today so the units can ship
per the EndDate already on the order. The Pune Power Lab is queuing a
calibration acceptance window the day after delivery, so any further hold
will slip our calibration schedule.

Attached are the wire confirmation and the payment-proof worksheet from our
treasury team.

Regards,
Rituraj Singh
Procurement Lead, Leeway Hertz
rituraj@leewayhertz.com

Detect whether a CSR has typed override instructions in this email. Return JSON only.

override_instruction.description: Verbatim CSR instruction text extracted from the email (empty string if none).

override_intent.description: If override_kind=intent_override, the canonical intent the CSR named. Empty string otherwise.

override_track.description: If override_kind=force_track, the track the CSR named. Empty string otherwise.

override_team.description: If override_kind=route_to_team, the named team (e.g., 'legal', 'service', 'credit'). Empty string otherwise.

reasoning.description: 1 sentence quoting the CSR instruction and explaining why it overrides the classifier.

raw_response: {"has_override":false,"override_kind":"none","override_instruction":"","override_intent":"","override_track":"","override_team":"","reasoning":"The email is directly from the customer requesting a hold release and contains no internal CSR-added directive above a forwarded-message delimiter.","confidence":0.93}

parsed.reasoning: The email is directly from the customer requesting a hold release and contains no internal CSR-added directive above a forwarded-message delimiter.
